description: A data/logic hook pair regenerates the pack when a schema, CONTEXT, or ADR source is newer-or-equal than the pack; default-off in hooks.config.json, gated by featureEnabled, logging one invocation per firing, exiting 0 on any error, and writing the pack from the hook's own process — never blocking the triggering Write.
title: Feature-flagged staleness hook regenerates the Grounding Pack
acceptance_criteria: A new grounding-pack feature flag in hooks.config.json defaults false; HOOK_FEATURES and the hooks-config schema carry it
With the flag off the hook is a logged no-op (OUTCOME_DISABLED) and writes no pack
With a deliberately broken regenerator the hook exits 0, logs the error outcome, and does not block the triggering Write
The hook writes the pack from its own process (not Claude's Write tool), so PostToolUse does not re-enter
Staleness uses a >= mtime comparison with a content-hash fallback on the CONTEXT and ADR sources
